name: Farmers Bank & Trust
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank based in Great Bend, Kansas offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Farmers Bank & Trust is a community-based financial institution headquartered in Great Bend, Kansas. The bank has established itself as a regional banking provider serving both personal and business customers across Kansas. As an FDIC-insured institution, customer deposits are protected by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing foundational security for depositors.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including personal checking, SafeGuard Checking, savings accounts, health savings accounts, and various loan products. For business customers, Farmers Bank & Trust provides business checking accounts, cash management solutions, agricultural and business loans, and business card services. Their digital offerings include mobile banking through the FBTMobile App (which displays credit scores), online banking, mobile deposit, Zelle money transfers, online bill pay, and e-statements. The institution also provides home financing services with loan officers offering personalized guidance through the mortgage process.

Farmers Bank & Trust distinguishes itself through accessibility features (screen reader compatibility, keyboard navigation), localized service with physical branch locations in the Great Bend area, and a focus on agricultural lending through dedicated Ag & Business Loan products. Their routing number (101107080) and NMLS #613839 confirm regulatory registration. The bank emphasizes security education and fraud prevention, offering resources on scams and online protection.

As a regional community bank, Farmers Bank & Trust is most suitable for customers seeking traditional banking relationships with local branch access rather than digital-only banking. The bank's offerings are standard for mid-sized community banks, and customers should compare rates and fees against larger regional or national institutions, as specific APR/APY information is not publicly listed on the website. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government
Free mobile deposit with FBTMobile App
Credit score access built into mobile app
Agricultural lending expertise through Ag & Business Loans
Zelle integration for peer-to-peer money transfers
Accessibility features for visually impaired users
Multiple e-services (online banking, mobile banking, e-statements)
cons: No specific interest rates or APY/APR information disclosed on website
Limited to geographic service area around Great Bend, Kansas
No information about minimum account balances or monthly fees
No mention of overdraft protection or fee structures
Website does not indicate 24/7 customer support availability
best_for: Kansas-based individuals and businesses seeking local banking relationships
Agricultural businesses and farmers requiring specialized lending
Customers who value in-person branch service over digital-only banking
Depositors prioritizing FDIC insurance and established community institutions
diagnosis: Farmers Bank & Trust is best for Kansas residents and agricultural businesses seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection and local branch service. The main caveat is the lack of publicly available rate information and geographic limitation to the Great Bend area—customers should contact the bank directly for current pricing and verify service availability before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
